The first thing is looking at, okay, when you're starting to work with a client, we need to really make sure that we nail the brand tone of voice and also the tone of voice of the personas, so the audience that they're working with. If we get that wrong, the client will just keep coming back to us for revisions and they're going to get frustrated, you're going to get frustrated, it'll feel like you're off the mark, they're going to pull the rug under you, etc. The first thing you need to do is make sure that you have a really comprehensive brief for your copy. Some of the things you want to make sure that you ask for in there are in addition to the normal things like who's your audience, what's the price point, the offer, etc. This is all stuff that should already be in a briefing document already, but additionally to that, you want to start talking about potential personas within the audience. ## [03:01 - 06:02]

100K plus a month, right? And each one of those personas, while they fit under your audience, will have very different needs and wants. They have very different problems. It's a lot easier doing it with your client than trying to guess all of this yourself, by the way, guys. Don't be afraid to spend some time talking with your client about this and strategizing. It should just be part of the onboarding process. They will really appreciate that as well. What you also want to ask the client is what is the rational thing that this product solves?
